commit.message:
Use skeletonization preprocessing for much better accuracy

Canvas strokes are thick (mouse/finger drawing), causing "3" to look
like "8" after resize. Now uses scikit-image skeletonization to:
1. Reduce all strokes to 1px skeleton
2. Dilate to consistent EMNIST-like width
3. Gaussian blur for smooth edges

Tested accuracy (canvas simulation, 200+ samples each):
- Digits mode: 96.0%
- Letters mode: 87.0%
- All mode: 80.0%

Also added scikit-image to requirements.txt.

commit.message:
[API] Add per-context timeout to /gpu-metrics endpoint (#9044)

* [API] Add per-context timeout to /gpu-metrics endpoint

When a remote K8s cluster becomes unreachable, the /gpu-metrics endpoint
blocks on the hanging port-forward (up to 30s httpx timeout), exceeding
the Prometheus scrape_timeout (10s). This causes Prometheus to mark the
target as down (up=0) and eventually fire the TargetDown alert, even
though the endpoint itself is healthy for all other contexts.

Wrap each context's metrics collection with asyncio.wait_for(timeout=8s)
so that a single unreachable cluster cannot block the entire response.
The 8s timeout is chosen to be safely under the 10s scrape_timeout,
ensuring the endpoint always responds in time for Prometheus.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [API] Fix subprocess leak on per-context timeout cancellation

Reduce start_port_forward_timeout from 10s to 5s so it fits within
the 8s per-context timeout with room for the HTTP request. Switch
the finally-block cleanup from async (asyncio.to_thread) to
synchronous so that CancelledError from asyncio.wait_for cannot
skip subprocess termination.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: handle DataType::Null in adjust_child_validity to prevent panic (#6160)

Previously, `adjust_child_validity` would call `ArrayData::try_new` with
a null bitmap on a `DataType::Null` array, causing an `.unwrap()` panic
with `InvalidArgumentError("Arrays of type Null cannot contain a null
bitmask")`.

The trigger: when a user inserts rows where a struct sub-field has only
null values, Arrow infers `DataType::Null` for that column. If a
subsequent fragment omits that nullable sub-field, Lance inserts a
`NullReader` to fill it in. `MergeStream` then merges the real batch
(with null struct rows) and the `NullReader` batch (all-null struct),
recursing into the struct where `adjust_child_validity` is called with
the `Null`-typed child and a non-empty parent validity — triggering the
panic.

Fix: skip the bitmask operation when `child.data_type() ==
DataType::Null`. A `Null` array is always entirely null by definition
and needs no validity adjustment.

Closes https://github.com/lance-format/lance/issues/6159

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: Use u64 copies in console framebuffer scroll

The scroll function was copying ~3MB of framebuffer byte-by-byte
with volatile accesses, causing multi-second delays. Switching to
u64-sized copies reduces memory operations by 8x.

commit.message:
ci: add one-shot workflow to enable Grafana iframe embedding (#1643)

## Summary
- Adds a `workflow_dispatch` workflow that patches the Grafana
deployment with env vars to enable iframe embedding
- Sets `GF_SECURITY_ALLOW_EMBEDDING=true`,
`GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=true`, `GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ORG_ROLE=Viewer`
- One-shot: delete this workflow after successful execution

## Context
After PR #1639 fixed the `.env.local` build issue, Grafana panels show
broken icons due to `X-Frame-Options: deny`. The root cause is Grafana's
default config — anonymous access is disabled, so iframes trigger a
login redirect chain where every response has `X-Frame-Options: deny`.

This workflow patches the live Grafana deployment via `kubectl set env`
to enable anonymous Viewer access and allow embedding.

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge and trigger workflow_dispatch
- [ ] Verify Grafana panels load on console.gostoa.dev/operations
- [ ] Delete workflow file after success

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: robust column width preservation across all save/load paths

Root cause: three bugs caused column widths to corrupt on save:
1. XML attribute regex assumed fixed order (Id before Target),
   but attribute order in XML is not guaranteed
2. Encrypted saves: xlsx-populate's final outputAsync re-corrupted
   column widths after fixExcelJSColWidths had fixed them
3. Encrypted file reopen: decryptIfNeeded didn't fix column widths,
   so savedOriginalColsXml captured already-corrupted data

Fixes:
- Add buildSheetNameMapping() utility with order-independent XML parsing
- Add restoreColWidthsInXlsxPopulate() to set correct widths in
  xlsx-populate's model before outputAsync (encryption step)
- Add captureColWidthsFromXlsxPopulate() to capture widths from
  xlsx-populate model before outputAsync corrupts them
- Fix decryptIfNeeded to restore column widths after decryption
- Refactor fixExcelJSColWidths to use buildSheetNameMapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Flip buy order logic: notional market first, limit as non-fractional fallback

Always attempt notional market buy first (supports fractional shares, simpler).
If Alpaca rejects with a non-fractionable error, fall back to a whole-share
limit order at price * (1 + limitPercent%). Removes pre-fetch price step and
the orderType setting — execution strategy is now automatic per ticker.

fix(governance-mcp): use `open` package for cross-platform browser launch

Replaces hand-rolled WSL2/macOS/Linux browser detection with the
battle-tested `open` npm package (18.7M dependents). Fixes
"Couldn't load XPCOM" error on WSL2 machines where Windows
executables aren't in Linux PATH — `open` resolves full PowerShell
path and uses wslpath for file conversion.

Works on WSL2, native Linux, macOS, and Windows without any
environment-specific configuration.

Fix backfill not running on redeploy + hide duplicate avg columns

Two issues:
1. session_state['backfill_done'] persisted from old broken code, so the
   new backfill was skipped on redeploy. Switched to module-level flag
   that resets on process restart.
2. 3d/7d columns showed identical values to 1d when insufficient data.
   Now shows blank when data span < 25h (3d col) or < 73h (7d col).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add lane-based spiral system for concurrent object streams

spiralPattern segments now support 'lanes' array for multiple
concurrent streams. Each lane has step (twist rate) and offset
(angular position). Objects distribute round-robin across lanes.
- Single step shorthand still works (backward compatible)
- Zero step = straight line, positive = CW, negative = CCW
- Enables: parallel lines, dual opposing spirals, mixed patterns
S1L1 demo: CW spiral → parallel lines → dual spirals → CCW finish

Fix backfill: parallelize exchanges, limit to top 30 symbols, run synchronously

The backfill was too slow (1200+ symbols, 3 workers, sequential exchanges)
and ran in a background thread so data wasn't ready for first refresh.

Fixes:
- Limit to top 30 symbols per exchange by |funding rate|
- Increase per-exchange thread pool from 3 to 20 workers
- Fetch all 4 exchanges in parallel
- Run backfill synchronously in Streamlit with spinner (not in collector)
- Fix KuCoin history parser: key is 'timepoint' not 'timePoint'

Result: 7-day backfill completes in ~50s with 11k+ rows.

commit.message:
Fix cash reserve double-deduction and unused order type in trade executor

- Remove cashReserve/cashMode from executeLiveTrades call: totalInvested is
  already the bot's allocated capital within adjustedEquity; passing cashReserve
  again subtracted it a second time from the bot's investment amount
- Extract orderType and limitPercent from executeLiveTrades options (were passed
  in but never destructured — all trades defaulted to market regardless of setting)
- Implement limit buy logic: fetch current prices before buy loop, compute whole-
  share qty and limit price (price * (1 + limitPercent%)), fall back to notional
  market buy if price unavailable

commit.message:
fix(helm): enable Grafana iframe embedding for Console (#1642)

## Summary
- Grafana returns `X-Frame-Options: deny` by default, blocking all
embedded `<iframe>` panels on the Operations Dashboard
- Add `security.allow_embedding: true` to remove the X-Frame-Options
header
- Add `auth.anonymous` with Viewer role so embedded panels render
without Grafana login

## Context
The Operations Dashboard (PR #1632) embeds Grafana panels via `/d-solo/`
iframes. The health check fix (PR #1637 + #1639) now correctly detects
Grafana as available, but the iframes are blocked by Grafana's default
`X-Frame-Options: deny` header.

## Post-merge action required
This is a Helm values file — merging alone does NOT deploy. Run:
```bash
helm upgrade kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack \
  -n stoa-monitoring -f deploy/observability/values-grafana-oidc.yaml
```

## Security note
Anonymous Viewer access allows anyone with proxy access (`/grafana/`) to
view dashboards without login. Console RBAC controls which sections are
visible per role (UX gate). For full security gating, Grafana
Organizations with per-tenant auth would be needed (out of scope).

## Test plan
- [ ] CI green
- [ ] After helm upgrade: `curl -sI https://console.gostoa.dev/grafana/
| grep x-frame` returns empty (no deny)
- [ ] Grafana panels load in iframes on /operations page

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent server crash caused by client-only class loading

Client-only classes (BlockStateModelLoader, EntityRendererRegistry, etc.)
were being loaded unconditionally during mod initialization, causing
dedicated server crashes. Guard these calls with client-side dist checks
for all loaders (Forge, NeoForge, Fabric) across all affected versions.

Also add server run configurations and gameDirectory separation for
NeoForge versions to enable server-side testing.

Closes #1

commit.message:
Add bridge traditions (Mahayana, Vajrayana, Neoplatonism, Vedanta) and strengthen connections (#34)

Adds 4 critical "hub" traditions that connect existing clusters:
- Mahayana: bridges Theravada → Chan/Zen/Tibetan
- Vajrayana: bridges Mahayana → Tibetan Buddhism + Hindu Tantra
- Neoplatonism: bridges Gnosticism → Christian Mysticism → Sufism → Kabbalah
- Vedanta: contextualizes Advaita within broader Hindu philosophy

Updates 6 existing traditions with new connections to bridge nodes.
Fixes prebuild script to use npx and chains into build command.
Total: 26 traditions.
